headerDisplay: Exposes contact sensors that close when the sun's azimuth and altitude fall within configured ranges and (optionally) a weather provider reports sunny conditions.
footerDisplay: Azimuth is measured in degrees from north (0° = north, 90° = east, 180° = south, 270° = west). Altitude is degrees above the horizon (0° = horizon, 90° = zenith). Negative altitudes mean the sun is below the horizon. Get a free OpenWeatherMap API key at https://openweathermap.org/appid. The UV Index provider requires a One Call API 3.0 subscription (free for 1000 calls/day).

pollInterval.title: Solar Sensor Poll Interval (seconds)
pollInterval.description: How often to recalculate the sun position (in seconds).

weatherProvider.title: Weather Provider
weatherProvider.description: Optional. Configure a weather provider to gate sensors based on sky conditions.

provider.title: Provider Type
provider.description: Which weather metric to use for determining sunny/cloudy.

apiKey.title: OpenWeatherMap API Key
apiKey.description: Get a free key at openweathermap.org. The UV Index provider requires a One Call API 3.0 subscription.

threshold.title: Threshold
threshold.description: For owmCloudCover: max cloud cover percentage (0–100, default 50). For owmUVIndex: minimum UV index to count as sunny (0–20, default 3).

sunnySwitches.title: Sunny Switches
sunnySwitches.description: Optional switches that can be toggled by HomeKit automations (e.g. from a light sensor) to indicate sunny conditions. If any switch is On or the weather provider reports sunny, sensors treat the sky as sunny.

name.title: Switch Name
name.description: The name shown in HomeKit. Must be unique.

name.title: Sensor Name
name.description: The name shown in HomeKit. Must be unique.

azimuthMin.title: Azimuth Min (°)
azimuthMin.description: Start of the azimuth window (degrees from north, clockwise).

azimuthMax.title: Azimuth Max (°)
azimuthMax.description: End of the azimuth window. If min > max the range wraps through north (0°).
